meta.title: ENG 1–2 ARG · A Semi-final in Data
meta.description: England 1-2 Argentina, FIFA World Cup 2026 semi-final, told minute by minute through data.

hero.subHtml: The defending champions, from the brink again. Anthony Gordon put England ahead in Atlanta — then Lionel Messi conjured Argentina's last-gasp turnaround: two goals in the final seven minutes.

timeline.heading: How it happened
timeline.lede: England led for thirty minutes. Argentina needed seven to end it. The scroll bar is your match clock.

events.title: A home game in sky blue
events.textHtml: Atlanta Stadium, and Argentina's fans vastly outnumber England's. The champions arrive having come from behind in every knockout match of their run. England arrive off the Bellingham night against Norway — with referee Ismail Elfath in the middle.

events.title: Fouls, and not much football
events.textHtml: A physical, disorganised opening: 19 combined fouls and zero shots on target by the hydration break. Elliot Anderson takes the game's first booking for a foul on Messi, and a John Stones header is the closest anyone comes.

events.title: Gordon breaks the deadlock
events.textHtml: Harry Kane works it wide, Morgan Rogers delivers, and Anthony Gordon converts the cross. Against 64 per cent Argentine possession, England lead — and promptly retreat to protect it.

events.title: Enzo, from distance
events.textHtml: A short corner is worked back to Enzo Fernández, who beats Pickford from long range. Thirty minutes of English backpedalling undone by one swing of the boot, and the stadium erupts.

events.title: Lautaro, from Messi's cross
events.textHtml: Deep in stoppage time, Messi shapes a perfect cross and Lautaro Martínez finishes it. From 1–0 down to 2–1 up in seven minutes — the comeback champions do it again.

events.title: The champions march on
events.textHtml: Nine minutes of stoppage time, England suddenly the ones chasing, Argentina seeing it out. The 2022 winners reach another final. England's wait goes on.

stats.heading: The numbers behind it
stats.lede: One-sided in a way the scoreline flatters. Argentina had the ball, the chances and the patience. England had one cross — and thirty good minutes.
stats.note: Source: FIFA match centre & ESPN match data, 15 July 2026. Figures over 90 minutes.

charts.heading: The match in charts
charts.lede: Hover or tap anything below. Every segment is the real feed data.

radar.title: Head to head · six-axis radar
radar.sideHtml: Each axis is scaled to whichever team led it, so the outer edge means best in match. Argentina's shape swallows almost the whole chart — possession, passing, chances. England stayed level on duels, and almost nothing else.
radar.note: Click a team to isolate the other's shape. Hover any vertex for the raw number.

controversy.heading: Two calls England will replay forever

cards.title: The retreat after 1–0
cards.textHtml: From the 55th minute England barely crossed halfway, inviting wave after wave of Argentine pressure onto a back line that had defended superbly all tournament. It held for thirty minutes. It needed thirty-five.

cards.title: You cannot sit off him
cards.textHtml: Given the freedom of a deep block to play against, Messi engineered the turnaround — the corner routine that freed Enzo Fernández, then the stoppage-time cross Lautaro Martínez buried. Argentina have trailed in every knockout round. They've won them all.
